pattern: An optimal carpet always starts flush with the left edge of some tile range, so a two-pointer sweep over sorted ranges with prefix sums evaluates every candidate start.
hints: Sliding the carpet left until its left edge hits a tile's start never loses coverage, so only those starts matter.
Sort the ranges and keep prefix sums of their lengths so you can total fully-covered ranges in O(1).
For each range as the start, advance a right pointer over ranges that finish within reach, add the prefix-summed full coverage, then add the partial overlap of the boundary range.

edge_inputs.description: carpet spans several ranges plus a partial one
carpet covers a far range fully
single range fully covered by a long carpet
carpet shorter than a single range covers only part
carpet length one covers a single tile
best start is not the first range
carpet boundary cuts through a range partially

stress.description: many non-overlapping ranges with a moderate carpet length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    tiles = []
    cur = rng.randint(1, 100)
    for _ in range(n):
        ln = rng.randint(0, 20)
        l = cur
        r = cur + ln
        tiles.append([l, r])
        cur = r + rng.randint(1, 20)
    carpet = rng.randint(1, 200)
    return [tiles, carpet]
stress.note: non-overlapping ranges built by always advancing past the previous end
